conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical point conditions for PEG were established by analyzing a series of PEG narrow standards of different molecular weights using different isocratic combinations of acetonitrile and water.
conditions.evidence_text: Analysis of PEG Eluent: 49% Acetonitrile in Water... Critical point conditions for PEG were established by analyzing a series of PEG narrow standards of different molecular weights using different isocratic combinations of acetonitrile and water.
conditions.notes: Used to analyze amine end-group modified PEG. At critical point, elution is independent of molecular weight.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical conditions were established for both polystyrene and polymethyl methacrylate by running narrow standards of varying molecular weight using different isocratic mixtures of solvents.
conditions.evidence_text: Analysis of PMMA Eluent: Polystyrene - 47% ACN in THF... The poly(styrene-b-methyl methacrylate) sample was analyzed under the critical conditions for polystyrene. Using these conditions, the elution is controlled purely by the molecular weight of the methyl methacrylate block of copolymer, the polystyrene block is 'chromatographically invisible'.
conditions.notes: Critical condition established for Polystyrene block to render it invisible, allowing analysis of the PMMA block.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical conditions were established for both polystyrene and polymethyl methacrylate by running narrow standards of varying molecular weight using different isocratic mixtures of solvents.
conditions.evidence_text: Analysis of PMMA Eluent: Polymethylmethacrylate - 17% ACN, 17% Water, 66% THF... Under the critical conditions for PMMA, chromatography of PS resulted in elution based on adsorption mode.
conditions.notes: Critical condition established for PMMA block. Used to analyze the PS block (which elutes by adsorption under these conditions).
